_about: Reference targets for ddX-enabled PCM validation. PRODUCTION PCM PHYSICS IS FORTRAN-SIDE; these values are comparison targets only and never feed the SCF. The paper-facing closed-shell HF tier uses Born/ddX plus STANDARD (unscaled) PySCF ddCOSMO: both pass the analytic Born-ion oracle, while PySCF solvent.ddpcm fails it (it applies an extra f(eps)=(eps-1)/eps on top of its R_eps solve). The former f(eps)*ddCOSMO targets were circular (scaled by the same spurious factor the pre-fix OpenQP code applied) and were regenerated on 2026-06-12 after the f(eps) double-count was removed from e_pcm/V_pcm. PySCF ddPCM values are diagnostic/problematic comparators, not final hard gates. A row is a hard pass/fail gate when reference_value is non-null and status is 'verified'.

trusted_reference_regressions.description: ddPCM electrostatic solvation energy of a fixed 12-site point-charge model (no QM SCF). Validates the OpenQP<->ddX adapter numerics (model build -> solve -> esolv) only.
trusted_reference_regressions.source: source/solvent_ddx_adapter.c::oqp_ddx_run_point_charge_smoke (dense ddPCM, se=-1; value asserted in-source within 1e-6 at line ~191); printed as energy=<float> by tests/ddx_adapter_smoke.c

empirical_verification.note: Verified via real ddX build through oqp_ddx_adapter_smoke after the intentional dense/no-FMM and se=-1 protocol alignment. Full ddx_ddrun lifecycle and explicit setup/solve lifecycle agree to ~4.8e-14. FD check: |dE/dphi0-(-0.5 q_cav0)|~6.6e-09 vs |dE/dphi0-q_cav0|~9.1e-05, confirming chg=-0.5*q_cav.

trusted_reference_regressions.validates: ddX model allocation, electrostatics, forward/adjoint solve, and esolv numerics through the OpenQP-owned C adapter
trusted_reference_regressions.does_not_validate: QM SCF coupling -- electronic contribution to psi, q_cav sign/scale into the Fock operator, phi_cav-from-density convention, and e_pcm vs 0.5*Tr[D.V] bookkeeping all remain open and require the Tier-2 QM benchmarks below

benchmarks.reference_source: PySCF STANDARD (unscaled) ddCOSMO reaction-field energy under the matched protocol (eps=78.3553, lmax=8, Lebedev 302, eta=0.1, Bondi-by-Z radii; spherical 6-31G*). Standard ddCOSMO passes the analytic Born-ion oracle, as does OpenQP's native ddX/ddPCM path after the f(eps) double-count fix; the former f(eps)*ddCOSMO reference was circular (scaled by the same spurious factor as the pre-fix OpenQP energy) and is retired. See /bighome/alireza/Projects/MRSF-PCM/pcm_rhf_rohf_unscaled_ddcosmo_post_feps_fix_20260612_072540/results/report_unscaled_ddcosmo.md and /bighome/alireza/Projects/MRSF-PCM/pcm_rhf_rohf_unscaled_ddcosmo_post_feps_fix_20260612_072540/results/openqp_vs_pyscf_unscaled_ddcosmo_comparison.csv.

benchmarks.independent_validation: OpenQP - unscaled PySCF ddCOSMO = +0.055 kcal/mol (post f(eps) fix; both sides pass the Born-ion oracle). Consistency check: old f(eps)-scaled reference / f(eps) agrees with the fresh ddCOSMO run to 5.99e-14 Ha.
benchmarks.openqp_e_pcm_provenance: native OpenQP ddX/ddPCM full-density source projection, physical energy convention e_pcm = -0.5*<phi_cav,q_cav> (NO dielectric factor; ddX folds the eps response into its R_eps operators, Born-ion oracle verified)

benchmarks.reference_source: PySCF STANDARD (unscaled) ddCOSMO reaction-field energy for BHHLYP/6-31G* under the matched protocol (eps=78.3553, lmax=8, Lebedev 302, eta=0.1, Bondi-by-Z radii; spherical 6-31G*), from the full-density DFT-PCM validation run MRSF-PCM/dft_pcm_check_20260613_233255. Standard ddCOSMO passes the analytic Born-ion oracle; OpenQP full-density ddX/ddPCM matches it for DFT to the same accuracy as the HF tier (DFT set MAE 0.027 (PBE)/0.028 (BHHLYP) kcal/mol).

PySCF solvent.ddpcm is kept only as a diagnostic/problematic comparator because it fails the Born-ion oracle under this protocol.
